section_name: Sign required on privately-owned vehicle used in delivery of mail
text:
(1) Every person operating a privately-owned vehicle in delivering the United States mail to the addressee thereof shall affix to such vehicle a white sign, visible from the rear, containing the following statement: U. S. MAIL Such statement shall be in black lettering at least four inches in height.

(2) Every person who violates the provisions of this section shall be punishable by a fine not exceeding twenty-five dollars or by imprisonment for not more than ten days or by both such fine and imprisonment.
markdown:
# Vehicle & Traffic Law
## Title 7
#### Article 33. Misc. Rules
##### § 1229 — Sign required on privately-owned vehicle used in delivery of mail

(1) Every person operating a privately-owned vehicle in delivering the United States mail to the addressee thereof shall affix to such vehicle a white sign, visible from the rear, containing the following statement: U. S. MAIL Such statement shall be in black lettering at least four inches in height.

(2) Every person who violates the provisions of this section shall be punishable by a fine not exceeding twenty-five dollars or by imprisonment for not more than ten days or by both such fine and imprisonment.

*Last modified: Sep. 22, 2014*

Every person who violates the provisions of this section shall be punishable by a fine not exceeding twenty-five dollars or by imprisonment for not more than ten days or by both such fine and imprisonment.
